title: On Respecting the Form of the Lord
summary: Krishna teaches Uddhava about witness consciousness, detachment from family/objects, and devotion (Bhakti). Addresses relationship endings, dealing with grief, letting go of attachments, and mental calm.

sections.summary: Themes: action and duty, self-knowledge, devotion. The passage moves from “S'r Uddhava said: 'Can You please explain the yoga of rites [kriy-yoga], the yoga of the service unto You as a deity, oh Master? Who is of that…” through “To be freed from his karma, he perfectly convinced should engage in My ritual worship [pj] and thereto perform duties, as prescribed in the…” through “After having assigned mantras to his own body and also having done this to My form, he should clean My Deity with his hand and properly prepare…”

Themes: self-knowledge, devotion, mind and meditation. The passage moves from “With the water of the vessel sprinkling the area of the deity, the utensils and his own body, he next should prepare three vessels with water…” through “Every day [the deity] should be bathed, as far as the means permit, using different kinds of water scented with sandalwood, us'ra root, camphor,…” through “Meditating in worship of Me as being brilliant, with a color of molten gold, with My conch, disc, club and lotus and with My four arms and…”

Themes: self-knowledge, devotion, mind and meditation. The passage moves from “Meditating in worship of Me as being brilliant, with a color of molten gold, with My conch, disc, club and lotus and with My four arms and…” through “Whenever one develops faith in Me, in whatever deity form or other manifestation, one should for that form be of worship, since I, the Original…” through “Both the perpetrator and his accomplice, the one who instigated it and the one who approved it, all will have to share, over and over, the…”

verses.sanskrit: श्री-उद्धव उवाच क्रिया-योगं समाचक्ष्व भवद्-आराधनं प्रभो । यस्मात् त्वां ये यथार्चन्ति सात्वताः सात्वतर्षभ
verses.transliteration: śrī-uddhava uvāca kriyā-yogaṃ samācakṣva bhavad-ārādhanaṃ prabho / yasmāt tvāṃ ye yathārcanti sātvatāḥ sātvatarṣabha
verses.translation: S'r Uddhava said: 'Can You please explain the yoga of rites [kriy-yoga], the yoga of the service unto You as a deity, oh Master? Who is of that worship, in respect of what form is one of worship and in what manner are You worshiped then, oh Master of the Stvatas [see also mrti and 11.3: 48-55]?

verses.sanskrit: एतद् वदन्ति मुनयो मुहुर् निःश्रेयसं नृणाम् । नारदो भगवान् व्यास आचार्यो ऽङ्गिरसः सुतः
verses.transliteration: etad vadanti munayo muhur niḥśreyasaṃ nṛṇām / nārado bhagavān vyāsa ācāryo 'ṅgirasaḥ sutaḥ
verses.translation: The sages Nrada, Bhagavn Vysa and my preceptor the son of Angir [Brihaspati], repeatedly say that for the welfare of men there is nothing as conducive.

verses.sanskrit: निःसृतं ते मुखाम्भोजाद् यद् आह भगवान् अजः । पुत्रेभ्यो भृगु-मुख्येभ्यो देव्यै च भगवान् भवः
verses.transliteration: niḥsṛtaṃ te mukhāmbhojād yad āha bhagavān ajaḥ / putrebhyo bhṛgu-mukhyebhyo devyai ca bhagavān bhavaḥ
verses.translation: The words about this that emanated from Your lotus mouth were spoken by the great unborn Lord [Brahm] unto his sons headed by Bhrigu, as also by the great Lord S'iva speaking to the goddess [Prvat, see B.G. 3: 9-10]. This [service to Your deity form] is approved by all classes and spiritual orders of society and is, I think, most beneficial for women and the working class, oh Magnanimous One.

verses.sanskrit: एतद् वै सर्व-वर्णानाम् आश्रमाणां च सम्मतम् । श्रेयसाम् उत्तमं मन्ये स्त्री-शूद्राणां च मान-द
verses.transliteration: etad vai sarva-varṇānām āśramāṇāṃ ca sammatam / śreyasām uttamaṃ manye strī-śūdrāṇāṃ ca māna-da
verses.translation: The words about this that emanated from Your lotus mouth were spoken by the great unborn Lord [Brahm] unto his sons headed by Bhrigu, as also by the great Lord S'iva speaking to the goddess [Prvat, see B.G. 3: 9-10]. This [service to Your deity form] is approved by all classes and spiritual orders of society and is, I think, most beneficial for women and the working class, oh Magnanimous One.

verses.sanskrit: एतत् कमल-पत्राक्ष कर्म-बन्ध-विमोचनम् । भक्ताय चानुरक्ताय ब्रूहि विश्वेश्वरेश्वर
verses.transliteration: etat kamala-patrākṣa karma-bandha-vimocanam / bhaktāya cānuraktāya brūhi viśveśvareśvara
verses.translation: Oh Lotus-eyed Lord, please, oh Controller of All Controllers in the Universe, speak to Your devoted bhakta about this means of liberation from the bondage of karma.'

verses.sanskrit: श्री-भगवान् उवाच न ह्य् अन्तो ऽनन्त-पारस्य कर्म-काण्डस्य चोद्धव । सङ्क्षिप्तं वर्णयिष्यामि यथावद् अनुपूर्वशः
verses.transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca na hy anto 'nanta-pārasya karma-kāṇḍasya coddhava / saṅkṣiptaṃ varṇayiṣyāmi yathāvad anupūrvaśaḥ
verses.translation: The Supreme Lord said: 'The number of [karma-knda] prescriptions for worshiping deities [see e.g. B.G. 1-6] is endless, Uddhava, let Me in brief explain it nicely one step at a time.

verses.sanskrit: वैदिकस् तान्त्रिको मिश्र इति मे त्रि-विधो मखः । त्रयाणाम् ईप्सितेनैव विधिना मां समर्चरेत्
verses.transliteration: vaidikas tāntriko miśra iti me tri-vidho makhaḥ / trayāṇām īpsitenaiva vidhinā māṃ samarcaret
verses.translation: One should worship Me as one desires following one of the three kinds of sacrifice according to the Vedas, the explanatory literatures [tantras like the Pacartra] or a combination of them.

verses.sanskrit: यदा स्व-निगमेनोक्तं द्विजत्वं प्राप्य पूरुषः । यथा यजेत मां भक्त्या श्रद्धया तन् निबोध मे
verses.transliteration: yadā sva-nigamenoktaṃ dvijatvaṃ prāpya pūruṣaḥ / yathā yajeta māṃ bhaktyā śraddhayā tan nibodha me
verses.translation: Now hear from Me how a person, who according to the for him specific Vedic precepts [*] achieved the status of a second birth, should worship Me with faith and devotion.

verses.sanskrit: अर्चायां स्थण्डिले ऽग्नौ वा सूर्ये वाप्सु हृदि द्विजः । द्रव्येण भक्ति-युक्तो ऽर्चेत् स्व-गुरुं माम् अमायया
verses.transliteration: arcāyāṃ sthaṇḍile 'gnau vā sūrye vāpsu hṛdi dvijaḥ / dravyeṇa bhakti-yukto 'rcet sva-guruṃ mām amāyayā
verses.translation: He must, connected in bhakti, free from ulterior motives ['honestly'] with the necessary paraphernalia worship Me, his worshipable guru, as being present in a deity, in a sacrificial area, a fire, in the [position of the] sun, in water or in the twice-born heart itself [**].

verses.sanskrit: पूर्वं स्नानं प्रकुर्वीत धौत-दन्तो ऽङ्ग-शुद्धये । उभयैर् अपि च स्नानं मन्त्रैर् मृद्-ग्रहणादिना
verses.transliteration: pūrvaṃ snānaṃ prakurvīta dhauta-danto 'ṅga-śuddhaye / ubhayair api ca snānaṃ mantrair mṛd-grahaṇādinā
verses.translation: For purification he should first bathe and brush his teeth, and next purify himself with both types of [Vedic and tantric] mantras while applying clay and such [see tilaka, kavaca and 6.8: 3-10].

verses.sanskrit: सन्ध्योपास्त्यादि-कर्माणि वेदेनाचोदितानि मे । पूजां तैः कल्पयेत् सम्यक्- सङ्कल्पः कर्म-पावनीम्
verses.transliteration: sandhyopāstyādi-karmāṇi vedenācoditāni me / pūjāṃ taiḥ kalpayet samyak- saṅkalpaḥ karma-pāvanīm
verses.translation: To be freed from his karma, he perfectly convinced should engage in My ritual worship [pj] and thereto perform duties, as prescribed in the Vedas [see also 11.14: 35], with worship and such [like expressing the Gyatr mantra] at the three junctures of the day [dawn, noon and sunset].

verses.sanskrit: शैली दारु-मयी लौही लेप्या लेख्या च सैकती । मनो-मयी मणि-मयी प्रतिमाष्ट-विधा स्मृता
verses.transliteration: śailī dāru-mayī lauhī lepyā lekhyā ca saikatī / mano-mayī maṇi-mayī pratimāṣṭa-vidhā smṛtā
verses.translation: There are eight types of forms with which one remembers Me: in stone, wood, metal, smearable substances [like clay], being painted, in sand, in jewels and as an image kept in mind.

verses.sanskrit: चलाचलेति द्वि-विधा प्रतिष्ठा जीव-मन्दिरम् । उद्वासावाहने न स्तः स्थिरायाम् उद्धवार्चने
verses.transliteration: calācaleti dvi-vidhā pratiṣṭhā jīva-mandiram / udvāsāvāhane na staḥ sthirāyām uddhavārcane
verses.translation: Of the two kinds of individual temple deities that are moved and not moved, the installed deity, oh Uddhava, is for His worship not taken away (udvsa) or again brought forward (vdane).

verses.sanskrit: अस्थिरायां विकल्पः स्यात् स्थण्डिले तु भवेद् द्वयम् । स्नपनं त्व् अविलेप्यायाम् अन्यत्र परिमार्जनम्
verses.transliteration: asthirāyāṃ vikalpaḥ syāt sthaṇḍile tu bhaved dvayam / snapanaṃ tv avilepyāyām anyatra parimārjanam
verses.translation: Not being installed one has these options, but when assigned a fixed place the following two possibilities are found: not being of a smearable substance [or being painted or made of wood] He is washed, in all other cases He is cleansed without water.

verses.sanskrit: द्रव्यैः प्रसिद्धैर् मद्-यागः प्रतिमादिष्व् अमायिनः । भक्तस्य च यथा-लब्धैर् हृदि भावेन चैव हि
verses.transliteration: dravyaiḥ prasiddhair mad-yāgaḥ pratimādiṣv amāyinaḥ / bhaktasya ca yathā-labdhair hṛdi bhāvena caiva hi
verses.translation: There is the worship of My different deities with excellent paraphernalia, there is the worship of a devotee free from material desire using whatever that is readily available, and there is the worship in the heart in a mental respect [by love and meditation only].

verses.sanskrit: स्नानालङ्करणं प्रेष्ठम् अर्चायाम् एव तूद्धव । स्थण्डिले तत्त्व-विन्यासो वह्नाव् आज्य-प्लुतं हविः
verses.transliteration: snānālaṅkaraṇaṃ preṣṭham arcāyām eva tūddhava / sthaṇḍile tattva-vinyāso vahnāv ājya-plutaṃ haviḥ
verses.translation: With a deity [in the temple] customary bathing and decorating is most appreciated, Uddhava, for a holy place that is an exercise of respect in mantras [tattva-vinysa] and for fire oblations [of sesame, barley etc.] drenched in ghee are considered best. With the sun that is an exercise of respect [with a sundial, see Srya-namaskar] and with water offerings of water and such are most suitable. Offerings presented with faith by a devotee of Mine are most dear to Me, even if it is just a bit of water.

verses.sanskrit: सूर्ये चाभ्यर्हणं प्रेष्ठं सलिले सलिलादिभिः । श्रद्धयोपाहृतं प्रेष्ठं भक्तेन मम वार्य् अपि
verses.transliteration: sūrye cābhyarhaṇaṃ preṣṭhaṃ salile salilādibhiḥ / śraddhayopāhṛtaṃ preṣṭhaṃ bhaktena mama vāry api
verses.translation: With a deity [in the temple] customary bathing and decorating is most appreciated, Uddhava, for a holy place that is an exercise of respect in mantras [tattva-vinysa] and for fire oblations [of sesame, barley etc.] drenched in ghee are considered best. With the sun that is an exercise of respect [with a sundial, see Srya-namaskar] and with water offerings of water and such are most suitable. Offerings presented with faith by a devotee of Mine are most dear to Me, even if it is just a bit of water.

verses.sanskrit: भूर्य् अप्य् अभक्तोपाहृतं न मे तोषाय कल्पते । गन्धो धूपः सुमनसो दीपो ऽन्नाद्यं च किं पुनः
verses.transliteration: bhūry apy abhaktopāhṛtaṃ na me toṣāya kalpate / gandho dhūpaḥ sumanaso dīpo 'nnādyaṃ ca kiṃ punaḥ
verses.translation: And what to say of an offering [by devotees] of foodstuffs, flowers, lamps, fragrances and incense [see also B.G. 9: 26]? An offer [by contrast] that, even if it is very rich, is made by a non-devotee, will not satisfy Me [see also B.G. 16].

verses.sanskrit: शुचिः सम्भृत-सम्भारः प्राग्-दर्भैः कल्पितासनः । आसीनः प्राग् उदग् वार्चेद् अर्चायां त्व् अथ सम्मुखः
verses.transliteration: śuciḥ sambhṛta-sambhāraḥ prāg-darbhaiḥ kalpitāsanaḥ / āsīnaḥ prāg udag vārced arcāyāṃ tv atha sammukhaḥ
verses.translation: Being cleansed he should then be of worship, having collected the necessary items, having arranged the seat with blades [of kus'a grass] pointing to the east and sitting down facing the east or the north, or else directly facing the deity [compare 1.19: 17, 4.24: 10, 8.9: 14-15].

verses.sanskrit: कृत-न्यासः कृत-न्यासां मद्-अर्चां पाणिनामृजेत् । कलशं प्रोक्षणीयं च यथावद् उपसाधयेत्
verses.transliteration: kṛta-nyāsaḥ kṛta-nyāsāṃ mad-arcāṃ pāṇināmṛjet / kalaśaṃ prokṣaṇīyaṃ ca yathāvad upasādhayet
verses.translation: After having assigned mantras to his own body and also having done this to My form, he should clean My Deity with his hand and properly prepare the sprinkling vessel and the sacred pot.

verses.sanskrit: तद्-अद्भिर् देव-यजनं द्रव्याण्य् आत्मानम् एव च । प्रोक्ष्य पात्राणि त्रीण्य् अद्भिस् तैस् तैर् द्रव्यैश् च साधयेत्
verses.transliteration: tad-adbhir deva-yajanaṃ dravyāṇy ātmānam eva ca / prokṣya pātrāṇi trīṇy adbhis tais tair dravyaiś ca sādhayet
verses.translation: With the water of the vessel sprinkling the area of the deity, the utensils and his own body, he next should prepare three vessels with water and arrange for the necessary auspicious items as far as available [like flowers, grains, blades of grass, sesame seeds etc., see ***].

verses.sanskrit: पाद्यार्घ्याचमनीयार्थं त्रीणि पात्राणि देशिकः । हृदा शीर्ष्णाथ शिखया गायत्र्या चाभिमन्त्रयेत्
verses.transliteration: pādyārghyācamanīyārthaṃ trīṇi pātrāṇi deśikaḥ / hṛdā śīrṣṇātha śikhayā gāyatryā cābhimantrayet
verses.translation: With the mantras for the heart [hridayya namah], the head [s'rase svh] and the tuft of hair [s'ikhyai vashath] the worshiper should purify the three vessels of water for His feet [pdya], His hands [arghya], and His mouth [camana], and do the same with the Gyatr.

verses.sanskrit: पिण्डे वाय्व्-अग्नि-संशुद्धे हृत्-पद्म-स्थां परां मम । अण्वीं जीव-कलां ध्यायेन् नादान्ते सिद्ध-भाविताम्
verses.transliteration: piṇḍe vāyv-agni-saṃśuddhe hṛt-padma-sthāṃ parāṃ mama / aṇvīṃ jīva-kalāṃ dhyāyen nādānte siddha-bhāvitām
verses.translation: He should meditate on the Original Individuality of all Expansions, the very subtle transcendental form of Mine that, within his body that was fully purified by air and fire, is situated on the lotus of the heart and by perfected souls is experienced in the end vibration of the Pranava [see also 2.2].

verses.sanskrit: तयात्म-भूतया पिण्डे व्याप्ते सम्पूज्य तन्-मयः । आवाह्यार्चादिषु स्थाप्य न्यस्ताङ्गं मां प्रपूजयेत्
verses.transliteration: tayātma-bhūtayā piṇḍe vyāpte sampūjya tan-mayaḥ / āvāhyārcādiṣu sthāpya nyastāṅgaṃ māṃ prapūjayet
verses.translation: With that [meditated form] by his own realization conceived as pervaded within his body, he, of worship fully being absorbed in thought of Me, should invite Me within the deity - and all that is respected along with it - by touching My limbs with mantras [nyasta] and thereupon honor Me [externally by performing pūjā].

verses.sanskrit: पाद्योपस्पर्शार्हणादीन् उपचारान् प्रकल्पयेत् । धर्मादिभिश् च नवभिः कल्पयित्वासनं मम
verses.transliteration: pādyopasparśārhaṇādīn upacārān prakalpayet / dharmādibhiś ca navabhiḥ kalpayitvāsanaṃ mama
verses.translation: After first having imagined My seat, decorated with the nine s'aktis and the [deities of] dharma etc. [*4] as an effulgent eight-petaled lotus with saffron filaments in its whorl, he should offer to Me the pdya, arghya and camana water and other items of worship to be perfect with the two [of enjoyment and liberation] in respect of both the Vedas and the tantras.

verses.sanskrit: पद्मम् अष्ट-दलं तत्र कर्णिका-केसरोज्ज्वलम् । उभाभ्यां वेद-तन्त्राभ्यां मह्यं तूभय-सिद्धये
verses.transliteration: padmam aṣṭa-dalaṃ tatra karṇikā-kesarojjvalam / ubhābhyāṃ veda-tantrābhyāṃ mahyaṃ tūbhaya-siddhaye
verses.translation: After first having imagined My seat, decorated with the nine s'aktis and the [deities of] dharma etc. [*4] as an effulgent eight-petaled lotus with saffron filaments in its whorl, he should offer to Me the pdya, arghya and camana water and other items of worship to be perfect with the two [of enjoyment and liberation] in respect of both the Vedas and the tantras.

verses.sanskrit: सुदर्शनं पाञ्चजन्यं गदासीषु-धनुर्-हलान् । मुषलं कौस्तुभं मालां श्रीवत्सं चानुपूजयेत्
verses.transliteration: sudarśanaṃ pāñcajanyaṃ gadāsīṣu-dhanur-halān / muṣalaṃ kaustubhaṃ mālāṃ śrīvatsaṃ cānupūjayet
verses.translation: One after the other he next must honor My disc-weapon [the Sudars'ana cakra], conch [the Pcajanya], club [the Kaumodak] and arrows and bow [the S'arnga], My [Balarma items of the] plow and pestle [hala and mushala], My gem [the Kaustubha], garland [the Vaijayant] and chest mark curl of white hairs [the S'rvatsa].

verses.sanskrit: नन्दं सुनन्दं गरुडं प्रचण्डं चण्डं एव च । महाबलं बलं चैव कुमुदं कमुदेक्षणम्
verses.transliteration: nandaṃ sunandaṃ garuḍaṃ pracaṇḍaṃ caṇḍaṃ eva ca / mahābalaṃ balaṃ caiva kumudaṃ kamudekṣaṇam
verses.translation: [He also honors] Garuda, Nanda, Sunanda, Pracanda and Canda, Mahbala, Bala, Kumuda and Kumudekshana [My carrier bird and eight associates].

verses.sanskrit: दुर्गां विनायकं व्यासं विष्वक्षेनं गुरून् सुरान् । स्वे स्वे स्थाने त्व् अभिमुखान् पूजयेत् प्रोक्षणादिभिः
verses.transliteration: durgāṃ vināyakaṃ vyāsaṃ viṣvakṣenaṃ gurūn surān / sve sve sthāne tv abhimukhān pūjayet prokṣaṇādibhiḥ
verses.translation: Durg, Vinyaka [Ganes'a], Vysa, Vishvaksena [see 6.8: 29, 9.21: 25-26], the spiritual masters and the demigods - each in their own place in front of the deity - should be worshiped with the sprinkling of water and other rituals [*5].

verses.sanskrit: चन्दनोशीर-कर्पूर- कुङ्कुमागुरु-वासितैः । सलिलैः स्नापयेन् मन्त्रैर् नित्यदा विभवे सति
verses.transliteration: candanośīra-karpūra- kuṅkumāguru-vāsitaiḥ / salilaiḥ snāpayen mantrair nityadā vibhave sati
verses.translation: Every day [the deity] should be bathed, as far as the means permit, using different kinds of water scented with sandalwood, us'ra root, camphor, kunkuma and aguru. Also hymns should be chanted, such as those from a section of the Vedas known as Svarna-gharma, the incantation called Mahpurusha, the Purusha-skta [from the Rig Veda] and songs from the Sma Veda, like the Rjana and others.

verses.sanskrit: स्वर्ण-घर्मानुवाकेन महापुरुष-विद्यया । पौरुषेणापि सूक्तेन सामभी राजनादिभिः
verses.transliteration: svarṇa-gharmānuvākena mahāpuruṣa-vidyayā / pauruṣeṇāpi sūktena sāmabhī rājanādibhiḥ
verses.translation: Every day [the deity] should be bathed, as far as the means permit, using different kinds of water scented with sandalwood, us'ra root, camphor, kunkuma and aguru. Also hymns should be chanted, such as those from a section of the Vedas known as Svarna-gharma, the incantation called Mahpurusha, the Purusha-skta [from the Rig Veda] and songs from the Sma Veda, like the Rjana and others.

verses.sanskrit: वस्त्रोपवीताभरण- पत्र-स्रग्-गन्ध-लेपनैः । अलङ्कुर्वीत स-प्रेम मद्-भक्तो मां यथोचितम्
verses.transliteration: vastropavītābharaṇa- patra-srag-gandha-lepanaiḥ / alaṅkurvīta sa-prema mad-bhakto māṃ yathocitam
verses.translation: My devotee should lovingly decorate Me with clothing, a sacred thread, ornaments, marks of tilaka, garlands and [apply] fragrant oils, the way it is enjoined.

verses.sanskrit: पाद्यम् आचमनीयं च गन्धं सुमनसो ऽक्षतान् । धूप-दीपोपहार्याणि दद्यान् मे श्रद्धयार्चकः
verses.transliteration: pādyam ācamanīyaṃ ca gandhaṃ sumanaso 'kṣatān / dhūpa-dīpopahāryāṇi dadyān me śraddhayārcakaḥ
verses.translation: The worshiper should with faith present to Me pdya and camana water, fragrances and flowers, whole grains, incense, lamps and other items.

verses.sanskrit: गुड-पायस-सर्पींषि शष्कुल्य्-आपूप-मोदकान् । संयाव-दधि-सूपांश् च नैवेद्यं सति कल्पयेत्
verses.transliteration: guḍa-pāyasa-sarpīṃṣi śaṣkuly-āpūpa-modakān / saṃyāva-dadhi-sūpāṃś ca naivedyaṃ sati kalpayet
verses.translation: According to his means he should make offerings of foodstuffs, like candy, sweet rice, ghee, rice flour cake [s'ashkul], sweet cakes [ppa], sweet rice flour dumplings with coconut [modaka], spicy sweet wheat cake of ghee and milk [samyva], yogurt and vegetable soups.

verses.sanskrit: अभ्यङ्गोन्मर्दनादर्श- दन्त-धावाभिषेचनम् । अन्नाद्य-गीत-नृत्यानि पर्वणि स्युर् उतान्व्-अहम्
verses.transliteration: abhyaṅgonmardanādarśa- danta-dhāvābhiṣecanam / annādya-gīta-nṛtyāni parvaṇi syur utānv-aham
verses.translation: On special days, or else every day, [the deity] should be offered a massage with ointment, a mirror, an eucalyptus stick for cleaning the teeth, a bath, food to be chewed and not to be chewed, as also song and dance.

verses.sanskrit: विधिना विहिते कुण्डे मेखला-गर्त-वेदिभिः । अग्निम् आधाय परितः समूहेत् पाणिनोदितम्
verses.transliteration: vidhinā vihite kuṇḍe mekhalā-garta-vedibhiḥ / agnim ādhāya paritaḥ samūhet pāṇinoditam
verses.translation: In a sacrificial area set up as prescribed he should, wearing a girdle, using a fire pit and an elevation for sacrificing, by hand build and bring to a blaze a fire that is equally piled up.

verses.sanskrit: परिस्तीर्याथ पर्युक्षेद् अन्वाधाय यथा-विधि । प्रोक्षण्यासाद्य द्रव्याणि प्रोक्ष्याग्नौ भावयेत माम्
verses.transliteration: paristīryātha paryukṣed anvādhāya yathā-vidhi / prokṣaṇyāsādya dravyāṇi prokṣyāgnau bhāvayeta mām
verses.translation: Spreading [kus'a grass mats] and then sprinkling and ceremonially [anvdhna] placing wood in the fire according to the rules, he should, having arranged for the camana water, sprinkle the items to offer and meditate on Me as residing in the fire.

verses.sanskrit: तप्त-जाम्बूनद-प्रख्यं शङ्ख-चक्र-गदाम्बुजैः । लसच्-चतुर्-भुजं शान्तं पद्म-किञ्जल्क-वाससम्
verses.transliteration: tapta-jāmbūnada-prakhyaṃ śaṅkha-cakra-gadāmbujaiḥ / lasac-catur-bhujaṃ śāntaṃ padma-kiñjalka-vāsasam
verses.translation: Meditating in worship of Me as being brilliant, with a color of molten gold, with My conch, disc, club and lotus and with My four arms and peacefulness; with My garment with the color of the filaments of a lotus, shining helmet, bracelets, belt, the ornaments on My arms, with the S'rvatsa on My chest, the effulgent Kaustubha and with a flower garland; while throwing pieces of wood soaked in ghee into the fire and in the course of the arghya ritual making the two offerings of sprinkling ghee [in two ways called ghras] and [two different] oblations of ghee [called jyabhgas], a learned person should, with root mantras and the [sixteen lines of the] Purusha-skta hymn, offer the oblations into the fire for Yamarja and the other demigods called Svishthikrit, in due order using a mantra for each [see also 11.14: 36-42, 11.19: 20-24, 11.21: 15].

verses.sanskrit: स्फुरत्-किरीट-कटक कटि-सूत्र-वराङ्गदम् । श्रीवत्स-वक्षसं भ्राजत्- कौस्तुभं वन-मालिनम्
verses.transliteration: sphurat-kirīṭa-kaṭaka kaṭi-sūtra-varāṅgadam / śrīvatsa-vakṣasaṃ bhrājat- kaustubhaṃ vana-mālinam
verses.translation: Meditating in worship of Me as being brilliant, with a color of molten gold, with My conch, disc, club and lotus and with My four arms and peacefulness; with My garment with the color of the filaments of a lotus, shining helmet, bracelets, belt, the ornaments on My arms, with the S'rvatsa on My chest, the effulgent Kaustubha and with a flower garland; while throwing pieces of wood soaked in ghee into the fire and in the course of the arghya ritual making the two offerings of sprinkling ghee [in two ways called ghras] and [two different] oblations of ghee [called jyabhgas], a learned person should, with root mantras and the [sixteen lines of the] Purusha-skta hymn, offer the oblations into the fire for Yamarja and the other demigods called Svishthikrit, in due order using a mantra for each [see also 11.14: 36-42, 11.19: 20-24, 11.21: 15].

verses.sanskrit: ध्यायन्न् अभ्यर्च्य दारूणि हविषाभिघृतानि च । प्रास्याज्य-भागाव् आघारौ दत्त्वा चाज्य-प्लुतं हविः
verses.transliteration: dhyāyann abhyarcya dārūṇi haviṣābhighṛtāni ca / prāsyājya-bhāgāv āghārau dattvā cājya-plutaṃ haviḥ
verses.translation: Meditating in worship of Me as being brilliant, with a color of molten gold, with My conch, disc, club and lotus and with My four arms and peacefulness; with My garment with the color of the filaments of a lotus, shining helmet, bracelets, belt, the ornaments on My arms, with the S'rvatsa on My chest, the effulgent Kaustubha and with a flower garland; while throwing pieces of wood soaked in ghee into the fire and in the course of the arghya ritual making the two offerings of sprinkling ghee [in two ways called ghras] and [two different] oblations of ghee [called jyabhgas], a learned person should, with root mantras and the [sixteen lines of the] Purusha-skta hymn, offer the oblations into the fire for Yamarja and the other demigods called Svishthikrit, in due order using a mantra for each [see also 11.14: 36-42, 11.19: 20-24, 11.21: 15].

verses.sanskrit: जुहुयान् मूल-मन्त्रेण षोडशर्चावदानतः । धर्मादिभ्यो यथा-न्यायं मन्त्रैः स्विष्टि-कृतं बुधः
verses.transliteration: juhuyān mūla-mantreṇa ṣoḍaśarcāvadānataḥ / dharmādibhyo yathā-nyāyaṃ mantraiḥ sviṣṭi-kṛtaṃ budhaḥ
verses.translation: Meditating in worship of Me as being brilliant, with a color of molten gold, with My conch, disc, club and lotus and with My four arms and peacefulness; with My garment with the color of the filaments of a lotus, shining helmet, bracelets, belt, the ornaments on My arms, with the S'rvatsa on My chest, the effulgent Kaustubha and with a flower garland; while throwing pieces of wood soaked in ghee into the fire and in the course of the arghya ritual making the two offerings of sprinkling ghee [in two ways called ghras] and [two different] oblations of ghee [called jyabhgas], a learned person should, with root mantras and the [sixteen lines of the] Purusha-skta hymn, offer the oblations into the fire for Yamarja and the other demigods called Svishthikrit, in due order using a mantra for each [see also 11.14: 36-42, 11.19: 20-24, 11.21: 15].

verses.sanskrit: अभ्यर्च्याथ नमस्कृत्य पार्षदेभ्यो बलिं हरेत् । मूल-मन्त्रं जपेद् ब्रह्म स्मरन् नारायणात्मकम्
verses.transliteration: abhyarcyātha namaskṛtya pārṣadebhyo baliṃ haret / mūla-mantraṃ japed brahma smaran nārāyaṇātmakam
verses.translation: Thus having been of worship he should bow to offer obeisances unto My associates and next present offerings chanting the basic mantra for the deity in question, thereby remembering Nryana as the Original Self of the Absolute Truth.

verses.sanskrit: दत्त्वाचमनम् उच्छेषं विष्वक्षेनाय कल्पयेत् । मुख-वासं सुरभिमत् ताम्बूलाद्यम् अथार्हयेत्
verses.transliteration: dattvācamanam uccheṣaṃ viṣvakṣenāya kalpayet / mukha-vāsaṃ surabhimat tāmbūlādyam athārhayet
verses.translation: After having offered camana water and giving the remnants of the food to Vishvaksena, he should present to Me prepared betel nut with fragrant substances for the mouth [see also 11.3: 48-53, 11.25: 28].

verses.sanskrit: उपगायन् गृणन् नृत्यन् कर्माण्य् अभिनयन् मम । मत्-कथाः श्रावयन् शृण्वन् मुहूर्तं क्षणिको भवेत्
verses.transliteration: upagāyan gṛṇan nṛtyan karmāṇy abhinayan mama / mat-kathāḥ śrāvayan śṛṇvan muhūrtaṃ kṣaṇiko bhavet
verses.translation: He should [next] for some time [see kla, 11.21: 9] become absorbed in My celebration, by listening himself - and make others listen - to My stories, by acting out My transcendental activities, by dancing, by chanting loudly and by singing along with others [see also e.g. 11.5: 36-37, 11.14: 23-24].

verses.sanskrit: स्तवैर् उच्चावचैः स्तोत्रैः पौराणैः प्राकृतैर् अपि । स्तुत्वा प्रसीद भगवन्न् इति वन्देत दण्ड-वत्
verses.transliteration: stavair uccāvacaiḥ stotraiḥ paurāṇaiḥ prākṛtair api / stutvā prasīda bhagavann iti vandeta daṇḍa-vat
verses.translation: With prayers from the Purnas, with large or small prayers from other ancient scriptures, with prayers written by others [see bhajans] and prayers from more common sources, he should prostrate himself, pay his obeisances and say: 'Oh Lord, please show Your mercy [prasda bhagavan].'

verses.sanskrit: शिरो मत्-पादयोः कृत्वा बाहुभ्यां च परस्परम् । प्रपन्नं पाहि माम् ईश भीतं मृत्यु-ग्रहार्णवात्
verses.transliteration: śiro mat-pādayoḥ kṛtvā bāhubhyāṃ ca parasparam / prapannaṃ pāhi mām īśa bhītaṃ mṛtyu-grahārṇavāt
verses.translation: Placing his head at My feet with his palms brought together [he may say a prayer like:] 'Oh Lord, please protect this surrendered soul who in this material ocean is afraid of being devoured by death [prapannam phi mm s'a, bhtam mrityu-grahrnavat, compare B.G. 11: 19].'

verses.sanskrit: इति शेषां मया दत्तां शिरस्य् आधाय सादरम् । उद्वासयेच् चेद् उद्वास्यं ज्योतिर् ज्योतिषि तत् पुनः
verses.transliteration: iti śeṣāṃ mayā dattāṃ śirasy ādhāya sādaram / udvāsayec ced udvāsyaṃ jyotir jyotiṣi tat punaḥ
verses.translation: Praying thus he should put the remnants granted by Me to his head and do this prayer once more - when the deity respectfully is to be bidden farewell - to give the light [of the deity] a place within the light [of his heart *6].

verses.sanskrit: अर्चादिषु यदा यत्र श्रद्धा मां तत्र चार्चयेत् । सर्व-भूतेष्व् आत्मनि च सर्वात्माहम् अवस्थितः
verses.transliteration: arcādiṣu yadā yatra śraddhā māṃ tatra cārcayet / sarva-bhūteṣv ātmani ca sarvātmāham avasthitaḥ
verses.translation: Whenever one develops faith in Me, in whatever deity form or other manifestation, one should for that form be of worship, since I, the Original Soul of All, am situated within My own form as also in all living beings [see also B.G. 6: 31 and *7].

verses.sanskrit: एवं क्रिया-योग-पथैः पुमान् वैदिक-तान्त्रिकैः । अर्चन्न् उभयतः सिद्धिं मत्तो विन्दत्य् अभीप्सिताम्
verses.transliteration: evaṃ kriyā-yoga-pathaiḥ pumān vaidika-tāntrikaiḥ / arcann ubhayataḥ siddhiṃ matto vindaty abhīpsitām
verses.translation: By thus being of worship with the [ritual] processes of acting in yoga as described in the Vedas and in more specialized texts, a person will, in both this life and the next, by My grace achieve the perfection he desired.

verses.sanskrit: मद्-अर्चां सम्प्रतिष्ठाप्य मन्दिरं कारयेद् दृढम् । पुष्पोद्यानानि रम्याणि पूजा-यात्रोत्सवाश्रितान्
verses.transliteration: mad-arcāṃ sampratiṣṭhāpya mandiraṃ kārayed dṛḍham / puṣpodyānāni ramyāṇi pūjā-yātrotsavāśritān
verses.translation: In order to properly establish My deity, the devotee should build a strong temple and maintain beautiful flower gardens [that provide flowers] for daily pj, festivals and yearly occasions.

verses.sanskrit: पूजादीनां प्रवाहार्थं महा-पर्वस्व् अथान्व्-अहम् । क्षेत्रापण-पुर-ग्रामान् दत्त्वा मत्-सार्ष्टिताम् इयात्
verses.transliteration: pūjādīnāṃ pravāhārthaṃ mahā-parvasv athānv-aham / kṣetrāpaṇa-pura-grāmān dattvā mat-sārṣṭitām iyāt
verses.translation: He who donates land, shops, cities and villages, so as to assure the continuance of the daily worship and the special occasions, will achieve an opulence equal to Mine.

verses.sanskrit: प्रतिष्ठया सार्वभौमं सद्मना भुवन-त्रयम् । पूजादिना ब्रह्म-लोकं त्रिभिर् मत्-साम्यताम् इयात्
verses.transliteration: pratiṣṭhayā sārvabhaumaṃ sadmanā bhuvana-trayam / pūjādinā brahma-lokaṃ tribhir mat-sāmyatām iyāt
verses.translation: Installing a deity one attains the entire earth, building a temple one attains the three worlds, and performing pj and likewise services one attains the realm of Brahm, but when one does all of these three, one will attain a quality [a transcendental integrity] equal to Mine.

verses.sanskrit: माम् एव नैरपेक्ष्येण भक्ति-योगेन विन्दति । भक्ति-योगं स लभत एवं यः पूजयेत माम्
verses.transliteration: mām eva nairapekṣyeṇa bhakti-yogena vindati / bhakti-yogaṃ sa labhata evaṃ yaḥ pūjayeta mām
verses.translation: He who free from ulterior motives worships Me thus, will by bhakti-yoga unite his consciousness in devotion and attain Me [see also 5.5: 14, 11.12: 24 and B.G. 6: 44].

verses.sanskrit: यः स्व-दत्तां परैर् दत्तां हरेत सुर-विप्रयोः । वृत्तिं स जायते विड्-भुग् वर्षाणाम् अयुतायुतम्
verses.transliteration: yaḥ sva-dattāṃ parair dattāṃ hareta sura-viprayoḥ / vṛttiṃ sa jāyate viḍ-bhug varṣāṇām ayutāyutam
verses.translation: The one who steals [or destroys] the property [and/or the service] delivered to the gods and the brahmins, whether given by himself or by others, is bound to take birth as a stool-eating worm for a hundred million years [compare 10.64: 39].

verses.sanskrit: कर्तुश् च सारथेर् हेतोर् अनुमोदितुर् एव च । कर्मणां भागिनः प्रेत्य भूयो भूयसि तत्-फलम्
verses.transliteration: kartuś ca sārather hetor anumoditur eva ca / karmaṇāṃ bhāginaḥ pretya bhūyo bhūyasi tat-phalam
verses.translation: Both the perpetrator and his accomplice, the one who instigated it and the one who approved it, all will have to share, over and over, the karmic consequences in the life that follows [depending the degree of the damage done].'
